title: klickd Payload v3.2
description: Normative schema for the .klickd inner decrypted payload. See SPEC.md v3.2 §20.2. This schema applies after successful AES-256-GCM decryption and authentication.

payload_schema_version.description: Payload schema version. MUST be '3.0' for payloads produced by a v3.0 encoder. Unrecognized values MUST trigger KLICKD_E_SCHEMA.

domain_schema_version.description: Domain-specific schema version in '{domain}-{major}.{minor}' format. Examples: 'education-1.2', 'work-2.1'. Unrecognized major versions SHOULD trigger KLICKD_E_SCHEMA.

injection_target.description: Where to inject the .klickd context. 'user_message' may improve verbatim recall for some models.

archived_sessions.description: Compressed summaries of past sessions. Active context stays in memory[]. Old sessions are archived here to bound file growth.

identity.description: Persistent user identity attributes. All sub-fields are OPTIONAL. See SPEC.md §8.

name.description: Human-readable display name or pseudonym. Maximum 256 Unicode code points.

language.description: BCP 47 language tag (e.g., 'en', 'fr-CA'). Used to configure language-sensitive agent behavior.

communication_style.description: Advisory preference for response style. Examples: 'concise', 'verbose', 'formal', 'casual'.

agent_instructions.description: System-prompt injection string. Intended for direct injection into the AI system prompt. Treated as an authoritative directive by consuming agents. See SPEC.md §7.2. Maximum 32,768 bytes (32 KiB) UTF-8 encoded. Exceeding this limit MUST result in KLICKD_E_FORMAT.

user_preferences.description: Advisory user preferences. MUST be treated as ADVISORY ONLY by consuming agents. An agent MAY override any preference for safety, legal, accuracy, or correctness. See SPEC.md §12.

avoid_topics.description: Topics the user wishes to avoid. Advisory; safety overrides apply.

locale.description: BCP 47 locale string. May overlap with identity.language.

context.description: Current operational state of the agent session. See SPEC.md §9.

current_state.description: Free-text description of the current working state or task. Maximum 2048 characters.

decisions_locked.description: Decisions that MUST NOT be revisited in the session. Each element is a plain-text decision statement.

ref.description: Reference URI or path to the artifact.

summary.description: Concise plain-text summary of the session context for agent priming. Maximum 4096 characters.

numerical_results.description: Key numerical results from the session. Agents MUST cite these values verbatim when resuming. Each entry is a {label, value, unit?, formula?} object.

interruption_point.description: Precise point at which the session was interrupted. Agents MUST resume from this exact point.

last_message_excerpt.description: Last 512 chars of the last message before interruption

resume_trigger.description: Exact phrase the agent MUST output at the start of a resumed session to signal continuity. Example: 'Reprise de la session du [date] — on en était à [topic] ([completion_pct]% terminé).'

mode.description: lightweight: minimal context for simple sessions (overhead reduction). full: complete context preservation (default).

language_switch_detected.description: Set to true if the user switched language between sessions. Context is language-agnostic and survives language switches.

subject_change_detected.description: Set to true if the user is starting a completely new topic. Agent SHOULD acknowledge the previous session is paused and create a new context branch.

knowledge.description: Structured knowledge state about the user or session subject. See SPEC.md §10.

mastered.description: Topics or skills the user has demonstrated mastery of. Maximum 500 entries.

gaps.description: Topics or skills identified as knowledge gaps. Maximum 500 entries.

struggles.description: Specific concepts the user struggled with. Agents MUST NOT re-explain already mastered content but SHOULD revisit struggles.

vocabulary_used.description: Domain-specific terms introduced and confirmed understood. Agents MUST reuse this exact vocabulary in resumed sessions.

next_steps.description: Recommended next actions or learning objectives. Maximum 100 entries.

memory.description: Structured conversation and event log. Maximum 1000 entries. Each entry MUST NOT exceed 10,240 bytes (UTF-8). Total array MUST NOT exceed 5,242,880 bytes (5 MiB). See SPEC.md §11.

ts.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp with Z suffix. Fractional seconds are OPTIONAL.

content.description: Text or serialized content of the memory entry. MUST NOT exceed 10,240 bytes when UTF-8 encoded.

tags.description: Optional categorization tags. Maximum 32 tags per entry. Each tag MUST NOT exceed 64 bytes (UTF-8).
